paragraph: Single-family homes in Leonia are moving in a real seller's market, with a median sale price of $867,500 and nearly two-thirds of homes selling above asking. The sale-to-list ratio sits at 1.02, and homes are spending a median of 21 days on market, which means priced-right inventory doesn't linger. You're looking at a range from $570,000 to $2.2 million, so the town draws everything from first-time buyers to people trading up significantly. Condos and townhouses, by contrast, tell a different story. With 11 sales at a median of $460,000, this tier is moving much more slowly at 53 days on market, and most sales are landing right at list price rather than above it. Only about 9 percent of condo sales went over asking, which suggests sellers in this category need to price competitively to attract serious interest. If you're choosing between single-family and condo in Leonia, the single-family market is hotter and more seller-favorable, while condos are offering buyers more negotiating room and lower entry prices.
